growth_records.strain: Continuous-bioreactor enrichment culture (inoculum: nitrogen-removal tank, hanseWasser municipal WWTP, Bremen, Germany)
growth_records.medium: Anoxic mineral medium with formate (electron donor/C source) and gaseous NO (sole electron acceptor)

up.method: reported (biomass/protein-specific NO reduction rate; volumetric 1.69 +/- 0.1 mmol NO/day, n=248)
reported (biomass/protein-specific formate consumption rate; volumetric 2.38 +/- 0.2 mmol formate/day, n=231)

sec.method: calculated: 2 NO -> N2, so N2 = NO_reduced/2 = 1.69/2 = 0.845 mmol/day (routine operation); NO reduced to N2 with virtually no N2O accumulation
reported (little to non-detectable N2O; virtually no accumulation)
reported (10-day experiment, n=6; net biomass production rate)
calculated by C balance: formate consumed 2.44 C-mmol/day minus 0.48 C-mmol/day fixed into biomass = ~1.96 mmol CO2/day oxidized (10-day experiment)

yields.Yxs_biomass_per_formate: 0.20 +/- 0.01 C-mmol biomass per mmol formate (n=6, 10-day experiment)

growth_records.cite: Garrido-Amador et al. Enrichment and characterization of a nitric oxide-reducing microbial community in a continuous bioreactor. Nature Microbiology (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41564-023-01425-8. [PMC10390337]
growth_records.snippet: Cells were then washed out at a dilution rate of ~0.104 per day, corresponding to a doubling time of 6.65 days. NO was reduced at a rate of 1.69 (+/-0.1; n=248) mmol per day (20.93 +/- 5.1 umol per mg protein per day) ... formate was consum
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D = dilution rate. D = medium flow / working volume = 250 ml/day / 2400 ml = 0.104/day (matches paper's stated ~0.104/day). Converted to per-hour: 0.104/24 = 0.004333/h. Doubling time reported 6.65 days = 6.65*24 = 159.6 h; check mu = ln2/td = 0.693/6.65 = 0.104/day, consistent. Substrate-specific uptake rates reported directly per mg protein per day (biomass-normalized): NO 20.93 +/- 5.1, formate 29.01 +/- 6.3 umol/mg protein/day; volumetric NO 1.69 +/- 0.1 and formate 2.38 +/- 0.2 mmol/day. Dedicated 10-day experiment (n=6): NO 1.57 +/- 0.02, formate 2.44 +/- 0.06 mmol/day; biomass 0.48 +/- 0.02 C-mmol/day; net yield Yxs = 0.48/2.44 = 0.197 ~ reported 0.20 +/- 0.01 C-mmol biomass per mmol formate. N2 secretion CALCULATED from stoichiometry 2 NO -> N2: 1.69/2 = 0.845 mmol N2/day (routine). CO2 secretion CALCULATED by carbon balance for 10-day run: formate C in (2.44) - biomass C (0.48) = 1.96 mmol C as CO2/day (paper states ~64% of formate linked to NO reduction/oxidation to CO2, ~20% assimilated to biomass, ~16% unaccounted). Conditions verbatim: 2.4 L working volume, 20 +/- 2 C, pH ~7.3 (1 M NaHCO3), stirred 500 rpm (two turbines), anoxic, NO sole e-acceptor. Single steady-state condition; routine-operation and 10-day-experiment rates emitted together as one record.
